scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: 16 read streams (contacts, campaigns, lists, segments, templates plus the wave2 5-stream base; domains, suppressions, suppressions_bounces, suppressions_complaints, suppressions_unsubscribes, webhooks, files, inbound_routes, sub_accounts, statistics_campaigns, statistics_channels new this pass) and 22 write actions covering contact/list/segment/template/campaign/webhook/domain/inbound-route lifecycle mutations. Excluded: security/apikeys and security/smtp-credential management (the create response returns the raw secret key/password material itself — a credential-issuance endpoint, not a syncable data resource; wiring it would put live secrets through record/fixture paths), the async bulk export/import job endpoints (contacts export/import, events export, suppressions bulk import — kickoff-and-poll jobs, not a synchronous read/write record operation), the transactional/raw email-send endpoints (POST /emails, /emails/transactional, /emails/mergefile — a one-shot send action outside this connector's contact/campaign/list data-management scope, not a syncable resource), the email-verification subsystem (single-address and bulk-file validation, a separate metered feature independent of contact/campaign data), single-object detail-by-id endpoints that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape, and non-data/account-introspection endpoints.

excluded.reason: single-contact detail; identical record shape to the contacts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

bulk multi-contact delete-by-email-list over the same collection DELETE /contacts/{email} already covers per-record; this dialect expresses the per-record mutation shape via delete_contact, not a bulk batch endpoint
asynchronous bulk-file contact import job (submit a file, poll for completion), not a synchronous single-record write
asynchronous bulk contact export job kickoff, not a synchronous read/write record operation
polls the status of an asynchronous export job this bundle does not kick off; not a syncable object stream
single-campaign detail; identical record shape to the campaigns list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-list detail; identical record shape to the lists list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
contacts scoped to one list; same record shape as the contacts stream (a per-list filter), not a distinct resource this dialect models as a second stream
bulk multi-contact removal from a list by email list; add_list_contacts covers the corresponding add-side mutation, the remove side is deferred as narrower breadth-vs-cost triage this pass
single-segment detail; identical record shape to the segments list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-template detail; identical record shape to the templates list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-domain detail; identical record shape to the domains list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

mutates DNS-verification-dependent domain settings (tracking type, VERP, custom bounce domain) that require the domain's DNS records already be correctly configured externally; a mismatched write silently fails DNS validation rather than erroring cleanly, and is narrower breadth-vs-cost triage than create/delete this pass
returns domain-restriction/compliance flags for a single domain, not an independently syncable object
triggers DNS re-verification of a domain's SPF/DKIM/DMARC/MX records against externally-configured DNS state; a caller-issued re-check with no data payload of its own, out of scope this pass
sets the default sending domain for a specific email/subaccount identity; a narrow account-configuration toggle, out of scope this pass
single-webhook detail; identical record shape to the webhooks list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-route detail; identical record shape to the inbound_routes list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

reorders the full set of inbound routes by submitting a complete ordered id list in one call; a whole-collection reorder operation this dialect's per-record write shape does not model, narrower breadth-vs-cost triage this pass
multipart file upload (the file's raw bytes as the request body), not a JSON record write this dialect's body_type dialect (json/form/none) can express
downloads the raw file bytes themselves, not a JSON-shaped record
single-file metadata detail; identical record shape to the files list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
file deletion write deferred this pass; the files stream already surfaces every file's metadata, narrower breadth-vs-cost triage
single-suppression detail; identical record shape to the suppressions list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

manually removing a bounce/complaint/unsubscribe suppression re-enables sending to an address that was suppressed for deliverability or compliance (e.g. CAN-SPAM/GDPR unsubscribe) reasons; a compliance-sensitive override, out of scope this pass
manually adds an address to the bounce suppression list ahead of any real bounce event; an operator override this pass defers
asynchronous bulk-file bounce-suppression import job, not a synchronous single-record write
manually adds an address to the complaint suppression list ahead of any real spam-complaint event; an operator override this pass defers
asynchronous bulk-file complaint-suppression import job, not a synchronous single-record write
manually adds an address to the unsubscribe suppression list, the same compliance-sensitive override concern as suppressions/{email} DELETE; out of scope this pass
asynchronous bulk-file unsubscribe-suppression import job, not a synchronous single-record write
provisions a brand-new billing sub-account under the caller's own account, including its initial password; an account-provisioning admin action outside per-record data mutation scope
single-subaccount detail; identical record shape to the sub_accounts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
permanently removes a billing sub-account and all of its own contacts/campaigns/data; an irreversible account-level admin action
adjusts a sub-account's billable email-sending credit balance; a billing/financial mutation outside this connector's data-management scope
changes a sub-account's sending/tracking configuration on the caller's behalf; account-administration scope, out of scope this pass
enumerates the account's own API credential metadata; a security-credential-management resource, not a business-data stream

the create response returns the newly issued raw API key value itself — routing live secret material through a declarative write/fixture/record path is a credential-exfiltration risk this connector's scope excludes
single API-key metadata detail; same security-credential-management concern as the list endpoint
mutates an existing API key's access level/IP restriction; a security-credential-management action outside this connector's scope
revokes an API key, potentially the very credential a caller depends on; an irreversible security action outside this connector's scope
enumerates the account's own SMTP credential metadata; a security-credential-management resource, not a business-data stream
the create response returns the newly issued raw SMTP password itself — the same credential-material concern as security/apikeys POST
single SMTP-credential metadata detail; same security-credential-management concern as the list endpoint
mutates an existing SMTP credential's access restrictions; a security-credential-management action outside this connector's scope
revokes an SMTP credential, potentially the very credential a caller's own outbound mail relay depends on; an irreversible security action outside this connector's scope
returns one account-wide cumulative-aggregate object for a date range, not a syncable per-record list
single-campaign statistics detail; identical record shape to the statistics_campaigns list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-channel statistics detail; identical record shape to the statistics_channels list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

raw per-recipient event-log stream (sent/opened/clicked/bounced timestamps); a large, high-volume analytics feed distinct from this connector's contact/campaign/list configuration-data scope, breadth-vs-cost triage this pass
single-transaction event detail; part of the excluded events log feed
per-channel event-log feed; part of the excluded events log feed
asynchronous bulk event-log export job kickoff, not a synchronous read/write record operation
polls the status of an asynchronous export job this bundle does not kick off; not a syncable object stream
asynchronous per-channel bulk event-log export job kickoff, not a synchronous read/write record operation
polls the status of an asynchronous per-channel export job this bundle does not kick off; not a syncable object stream
one-shot transactional/marketing email-send action; outside this connector's contact/campaign/list data-management scope, not a syncable resource mutation
one-shot transactional email-send action; same out-of-scope reasoning as POST /emails
multipart mail-merge send with an uploaded recipient file as the request body, not a JSON record write
delivery-status lookup for a single previously sent email; not modeled since the send action itself is out of scope
renders a single sent email's rendered HTML content for viewing; not a syncable object record
email-address-verification job history; the verification subsystem is a separate metered feature independent of contact/campaign/list data, out of scope this pass
single-verification-result detail; part of the excluded verification subsystem
submits a single email address for verification; part of the excluded verification subsystem
removes a verification result record; part of the excluded verification subsystem
multipart bulk-verification file upload, not a JSON record write; also part of the excluded verification subsystem
part of the excluded verification subsystem
part of the excluded verification subsystem
downloads a raw verification-result file, not a JSON-shaped record; also part of the excluded verification subsystem
part of the excluded verification subsystem
part of the excluded verification subsystem
